intro: Sonchus hierrensis is a rare species of thistle, belonging to the dandelion-like plants in the Asteraceae family, native to the island of Tenerife in the Canary Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Sonchus hierrensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to create remedies for digestive issues, such as stomach ulcers and irritable bowel syndrome.
